recentEvent: Hurricane Helene (2024) caused widespread outages across Miami-Dade County, with some neighborhoods losing power for over a week.
costVsState: 5-10% above the Florida average due to Miami-Dade's stricter wind code requirements and higher labor rates
localCode: Miami-Dade County enforces its own High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) code, which is stricter than the standard Florida Building Code. Generators must be rated for 175+ mph winds (vs. 150 mph statewide). Flood zone properties in low-lying areas require elevated mounting above the base flood elevation.
fuelReason: Most suburban Miami homes have natural gas via FPL's gas division. Propane is used in flood-prone areas where gas lines are restricted and in the Keys.
description: Miami sits in one of the most hurricane-exposed metro areas in the US. FPL serves over 5 million customers in Southeast Florida, and Miami-Dade's strict HVHZ building code adds $300-$500 to generator installations compared to other Florida counties. The combination of hurricane risk, extreme summer heat, and aging coastal infrastructure makes a standby generator close to a necessity here.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Miami?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW whole-house generator costs $9,500-$15,000 fully installed in Miami. This is 5-10% above the Florida average because Miami-Dade's HVHZ wind code requires rated mounting hardware that costs more than standard installations.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need HVHZ-rated mounting for my generator in Miami?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. Miami-Dade County is in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which requires all outdoor equipment to be rated for 175+ mph winds. This is stricter than the rest of Florida (150 mph). Your installer must use HVHZ-approved mounting brackets and fasteners.

faqSpecific.q: Natural gas or propane in Miami?
faqSpecific.a: Natural gas for most Miami homes. FPL provides gas service throughout the metro area. Propane is primarily used in flood-prone coastal areas, the Keys, and rural western Miami-Dade where gas lines don't reach.

recentEvent: Tampa Bay narrowly avoided a direct hit from Hurricane Milton (2024), but storm surge and wind caused significant outages across Hillsborough County.
costVsState: close to the Florida state average — Tampa has a competitive installer market
localCode: Hillsborough County follows the standard Florida Building Code. Flood zone properties along the bay and coast require elevated mounting. Tampa is the lightning capital of the US, so surge protection on the transfer switch is especially important.
fuelReason: TECO provides both electric and gas service in Tampa. Most suburban homes are connected to natural gas.
description: Tampa sits at the top of Tampa Bay, making it highly vulnerable to storm surge during hurricanes. The area is also the lightning capital of the US, with more lightning strikes per year than anywhere else in the country. TECO serves the Tampa metro, and outages from summer thunderstorms are a near-daily occurrence during the rainy season.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Tampa?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $9,000-$14,000 fully installed in Tampa. This is close to the Florida state average. Tampa's competitive installer market keeps pricing in check.

faqSpecific.q: Does Tampa's lightning risk affect my generator?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. Tampa gets more lightning strikes than any US metro. Your installer should include a whole-house surge protector on the transfer switch and the generator's control panel. Budget $200-$400 for proper surge protection.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need flood-rated mounting in Tampa?
faqSpecific.a: Only if your property is in a FEMA-designated flood zone (common along the bay and coastal areas). Hillsborough County's flood maps are available online. Properties in Zone A or V require elevated generator mounting.

recentEvent: Hurricane Beryl (2024) knocked out power to over 2 million CenterPoint Energy customers in the Houston metro. Some waited 10+ days for restoration.
costVsState: close to the Texas state average — Houston has the most installers in the state, creating strong competition
localCode: City of Houston requires an electrical permit for transfer switch installation. Harris County follows the NEC. Flood zone properties (common in Houston's bayou areas) may need elevated generator pads.
fuelReason: CenterPoint provides natural gas to most of the Houston metro. Gas is cheap and abundant in Texas.
description: Houston faces a triple threat: Gulf hurricanes, extreme summer heat, and the ERCOT grid vulnerability exposed by Winter Storm Uri. CenterPoint Energy, the local utility, has come under intense scrutiny after Hurricane Beryl (2024) left millions without power for over a week. Generator sales in Houston have surged every year since 2021.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Houston?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,500-$13,500 installed in Houston. This is close to the Texas average. Houston has the largest concentration of generator installers in the state, so getting 3 competitive quotes is easy.

faqSpecific.q: Is CenterPoint Energy reliable in Houston?
faqSpecific.a: CenterPoint has been heavily criticized for slow storm restoration. After Beryl (2024), over 2 million customers lost power, with some waiting 10+ days. Houston homeowners increasingly treat standby generators as a necessity, not a luxury.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need flood-rated mounting in Houston?
faqSpecific.a: If your property is in a FEMA flood zone (common along Buffalo Bayou, Brays Bayou, and low-lying areas throughout Harris County), yes. Elevated mounting adds $300-$600 to the installation.

recentEvent: Hurricane Ida (2021) knocked out power to all of New Orleans for over two weeks. All 8 Entergy transmission lines feeding the city failed simultaneously.
costVsState: close to the Louisiana average, though flood zone mounting requirements add $200-$600 in most neighborhoods
localCode: New Orleans requires permits through the Department of Safety and Permits. Most of the city is in a FEMA flood zone, requiring generators to be mounted above the base flood elevation. The city also requires specific setbacks from property lines in the historic districts.
fuelReason: Entergy provides gas service throughout New Orleans. Natural gas stayed available during Ida even when the electric grid failed completely.
description: New Orleans has the worst outage record of any major US city. After Hurricane Ida destroyed all 8 transmission lines feeding the city, every single customer lost power for 2+ weeks. Entergy's aging infrastructure means even tropical storms cause multi-day outages. If you live in New Orleans and don't have a standby generator, you're essentially planning to evacuate every hurricane season.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in New Orleans?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$12,500 installed in New Orleans. Flood zone mounting (required for most NOLA properties) adds $200-$600 to the base price.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need elevated mounting in New Orleans?
faqSpecific.a: Almost certainly yes. Most of New Orleans is below sea level and in a FEMA flood zone. Your generator must be mounted above the base flood elevation for your block. Your installer will check your flood zone and determine the required height.

faqSpecific.q: How long did Ida's outage last in New Orleans?
faqSpecific.a: All of New Orleans lost power when Ida hit in August 2021. Restoration took 11 days for the first customers and over 6 weeks for some neighborhoods. It was the worst single-utility outage event in US history.

recentEvent: Hurricane Helene (2024) caused massive flooding and power outages across the Charleston metro, even though it made landfall further south.
costVsState: slightly above the SC average due to coastal wind-rated mounting requirements and higher Lowcountry labor rates
localCode: Charleston County requires wind-rated equipment mounting per the SC Building Code coastal provisions. Flood zone properties (most of downtown Charleston and the islands) need elevated installations. Historic district properties may have additional placement restrictions.
fuelReason: Dominion provides gas service in the Charleston metro. Propane is common on the sea islands and rural areas.
description: Charleston's historic Lowcountry setting puts it at the intersection of every coastal hazard: hurricane winds, storm surge, and chronic flooding. Dominion Energy serves the area, and extended outages are increasingly common as storms intensify. The 2024 hurricane season was a wake-up call for many Charleston homeowners who had put off generator purchases.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Charleston?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $9,000-$14,500 installed in Charleston. Coastal wind-rated mounting and flood zone requirements add $300-$700 above inland SC prices.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need wind-rated mounting in Charleston?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. Charleston County is in the SC coastal wind zone, requiring all outdoor equipment to meet wind-speed design standards. This applies to all of Charleston, Mount Pleasant, James Island, and the barrier islands.

faqSpecific.q: Are there restrictions in Charleston's historic district?
faqSpecific.a: Historic district properties may face additional placement restrictions from the Board of Architectural Review (BAR). Generators typically need to be placed in rear yards and may require screening. Check with your installer about BAR requirements for your address.

recentEvent: A December 2022 winter storm caused widespread outages across the Charlotte metro, with Duke Energy implementing rolling blackouts to manage grid strain.
costVsState: slightly above the NC average due to Charlotte's higher labor rates as a major metro
localCode: Mecklenburg County follows the NC State Building Code. Standard electrical permit required. Charlotte's rapid growth means some newer subdivisions have HOA restrictions on generator placement — check before buying.
fuelReason: Piedmont Natural Gas serves the Charlotte metro. Most suburban homes have gas service.
description: Charlotte's Piedmont location means it gets hit by ice storms in winter and severe thunderstorms in summer. The city's massive growth over the past decade has strained Duke Energy's distribution grid. The December 2022 rolling blackouts were a shock to many Charlotte residents who assumed grid reliability was a given.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Charlotte?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $9,000-$15,000 installed in Charlotte. This is slightly above the NC average due to higher metro labor rates. Getting 3 quotes is especially important in Charlotte — price variation between contractors is significant.

faqSpecific.q: Do Charlotte HOAs restrict generators?
faqSpecific.a: Some do. Newer subdivisions often have HOA rules about outdoor equipment placement, screening, and noise. Check your HOA covenants before signing an installation contract. Most HOAs allow generators with proper placement — typically in the rear or side yard with screening.

faqSpecific.q: What causes Charlotte's power outages?
faqSpecific.a: Ice storms (winter) and severe thunderstorms (summer). Charlotte's tree canopy is dense, and falling limbs take down power lines regularly. Duke Energy has invested in tree trimming, but outages remain common during major weather events.

recentEvent: Hurricane Helene (2024) pushed inland through Georgia, bringing tropical storm-force winds to metro Atlanta and causing outages for over 500,000 Georgia Power customers.
costVsState: slightly above the Georgia average due to metro Atlanta's higher labor rates, but strong installer competition keeps it reasonable
localCode: Fulton County follows the IRC. Standard electrical permit required. Atlanta's dense tree canopy requires careful generator placement — your installer needs to position the unit away from large trees that could drop limbs during storms.
fuelReason: Georgia's deregulated gas market means competitive pricing. Atlanta Gas Light provides the infrastructure, with multiple marketers offering service.
description: Atlanta has one of the densest urban tree canopies in the US, which is beautiful but dangerous during storms. Falling trees and limbs are the #1 cause of power outages in metro Atlanta. Georgia Power serves the area, and outages from severe thunderstorms are a regular occurrence from March through September. Helene (2024) proved that even inland Atlanta isn't safe from hurricane-force events.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Atlanta?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,500-$14,000 installed in Atlanta. This is slightly above the Georgia state average due to metro labor rates, but below national average. Atlanta has a competitive installer market.

faqSpecific.q: How does Atlanta's tree canopy affect installation?
faqSpecific.a: Your installer must place the generator away from large oaks and pines that could drop limbs. Atlanta's dense canopy means outages happen frequently — and it also means your generator needs clear space above and around it for safe operation and ventilation.

faqSpecific.q: Natural gas or propane in Atlanta?
faqSpecific.a: Natural gas. Georgia's deregulated gas market means you can choose your gas marketer for competitive pricing. Atlanta Gas Light provides the infrastructure. Virtually all suburban Atlanta homes have gas service.

recentEvent: The January 2025 Palisades and Eaton fires forced evacuations across LA County. PSPS events in 2024 left hillside communities without power for 3-5 days during fire weather.
costVsState: at the top of California's range due to LA's high labor rates, complex permitting, and seismic + fire code requirements
localCode: LA requires seismic bracing (CBC Section 1613), city fire department clearance for properties in wildfire zones, and compliance with the LA Noise Ordinance (LAMC Section 112.05). Hillside properties face additional grading and setback requirements. LADWP and SCE service areas have different interconnection rules.
fuelReason: SoCalGas serves the LA metro. Some newer all-electric homes (California's push to electrify) require propane tanks for generators.
description: Los Angeles is the most expensive major metro for generator installation in the US. Between seismic bracing, fire department approvals, LA city permits, and labor rates, a standard 22 kW install runs $14,000-$23,000. But for homeowners in hillside and canyon communities where PSPS events cut power for days during fire season, the cost is justified by the first extended shutoff.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Los Angeles?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $14,000-$23,000 installed in LA — the highest of any major US city. Seismic bracing ($800-$1,500), fire department clearance, and LA city permits ($250-$600) all add up. Labor rates are 40-60% above national average.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need fire department approval in LA?
faqSpecific.a: If your property is in a Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone (VHFHSZ) — common in hillside and canyon areas — yes. LAFD requires clearance approval for generator placement to ensure it doesn't create a fire risk. This can add 2-3 weeks to the permitting timeline.

faqSpecific.q: Does LA's noise ordinance affect generators?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. LAMC Section 112.05 limits equipment noise at the property line. Most modern standby generators (65-67 dB at the unit) comply at typical setback distances, but if you have a small lot or close neighbors, your installer may need to add sound attenuation barriers.

primaryRisk: Ice storms and DTE grid failures
recentEvent: The August 2023 derecho knocked out power to over 800,000 DTE Energy customers in the Detroit metro. DTE's restoration took up to 7 days for some areas.
costVsState: close to the Michigan average — Detroit's labor rates are moderate and permitting is straightforward
localCode: City of Detroit requires an electrical permit for transfer switch installation. Wayne County follows the Michigan Residential Code. No unusual local requirements beyond standard NEC compliance.
fuelReason: DTE provides gas service throughout metro Detroit. Natural gas is cheap and universal in the area.
description: Detroit has a DTE problem. DTE Energy consistently ranks among the worst major US utilities for outage frequency and restoration time. The Michigan Public Service Commission has fined DTE multiple times for inadequate performance. For Detroit homeowners, a standby generator isn't about the next big storm — it's about the next random Tuesday when your power goes out for 8 hours because of aging infrastructure.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Detroit?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$12,500 installed in Detroit. This is close to the Michigan average. Detroit's moderate labor rates and straightforward permitting keep costs reasonable.

faqSpecific.q: How bad is DTE Energy's reliability?
faqSpecific.a: Bad. DTE ranks among the worst major US utilities for outage frequency. The Michigan PSC has issued multiple improvement orders. Average annual outage time for DTE customers is well above the national median. If you're a DTE customer, a generator is close to a necessity.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need my sump pump on the generator in Detroit?
faqSpecific.a: If you have a basement (most Detroit-area homes do), absolutely. Spring snowmelt + power outage = flooded basement. A sump pump draws 800-1,500W. Make sure it's on your essential circuits list when sizing your generator.

recentEvent: The March 2020 tornado outbreak killed 25 people and destroyed entire neighborhoods in Nashville. Tens of thousands lost power for days.
costVsState: close to the Tennessee average — Nashville's growing population is bringing more installers to the market
localCode: Davidson County follows the IRC with Tennessee amendments. Standard electrical permit from Metro Nashville Codes Department. Nashville's booming construction market means permit processing is efficient.
fuelReason: Piedmont Natural Gas serves Nashville. Most homes in Davidson County have natural gas service.
description: Nashville sits squarely in Dixie Alley, the new epicenter of US tornado activity. The devastating 2020 tornado outbreak was a wake-up call for the city. Tornadoes give zero warning for power outage preparation — your generator either starts automatically or you're in the dark. Nashville Electric Service is reliable by Tennessee standards, but no grid can withstand a direct tornado hit.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Nashville?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$13,000 installed in Nashville. This is close to the Tennessee average. Nashville's growing installer market means competitive pricing.

faqSpecific.q: Is Nashville really at risk for tornadoes?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. The March 2020 tornado outbreak killed 25 people and caused $1.6 billion in damage across Middle Tennessee. Nashville averages several tornado warnings per year. Tornadoes cause instant, widespread power loss with no preparation time.

faqSpecific.q: How fast does NES restore power after a storm?
faqSpecific.a: NES is generally efficient for a municipal utility. Typical restoration after a severe storm is 12-48 hours. After the 2020 tornado, some areas took 5-7 days. A standby generator covers you regardless of restoration timeline.

electricityProvider: Duke Energy Florida / OUC (Orlando Utilities Commission)
recentEvent: Hurricane Ian (2022) caused widespread outages across Central Florida. Over 700,000 Duke Energy customers in Orlando's service area lost power, with some neighborhoods dark for over 10 days.
costVsState: in line with the Florida statewide average — strong installer competition and moderate labor rates keep costs steady
localCode: Orange County requires standard Florida Building Code compliance for generator installations. No additional wind code beyond the statewide 150 mph requirement (unlike Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ). All permanent generator installations require an Orange County electrical permit.
fuelReason: Most Orlando homes have natural gas via TECO Peoples Gas. Propane is common in rural parts of Osceola and Seminole counties where gas lines don't reach.
description: Orlando sits in Central Florida's hurricane corridor. While not directly on the coast, tropical systems routinely push inland with destructive force. Hurricane Ian (2022) proved that being 50 miles from the coast doesn't protect you — over 700,000 Duke Energy customers lost power across the greater Orlando area. The city also holds the title of one of the most lightning-prone metros in the US.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Orlando?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW whole-house generator costs $9,000-$13,500 fully installed in Orlando. This is in line with the Florida average. Costs vary by neighborhood and distance from gas meter to generator placement.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need a hurricane-rated generator mount in Orlando?
faqSpecific.a: Orlando follows the standard Florida Building Code requiring 150 mph wind resistance. This is less strict than Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ code, so standard mounting hardware meets Orange County requirements.

faqSpecific.q: Which utility serves Orlando?
faqSpecific.a: It depends on your address. Downtown and parts of central Orlando are served by OUC (Orlando Utilities Commission), a municipal utility. Most surrounding areas are Duke Energy Florida. Check your electric bill to confirm.

recentEvent: Hurricane Matthew (2016) caused catastrophic flooding across Duval County with over 300,000 JEA customers losing power. Hurricane Irma (2017) followed a year later with additional widespread outages.
costVsState: slightly below the Florida average — lower cost of living and labor rates compared to South Florida metros
localCode: Duval County follows the standard Florida Building Code. JEA requires coordination for generator interconnection in some neighborhoods. Flood zone properties along the St. Johns River require elevated mounting.
fuelReason: TECO Peoples Gas serves most of Jacksonville's suburban areas. JEA also provides gas service in some parts of Duval County. Propane is used in rural Nassau and Clay county areas.
description: Jacksonville is the largest city by area in the contiguous US, and its sprawling layout means power restoration after storms takes longer than in compact metros. JEA, the municipal utility, serves the area. Jacksonville faces both Atlantic hurricanes and nor'easters, plus significant flood risk along the St. Johns River. The city's large military population (Naval Station Mayport, NAS Jacksonville) drives consistent demand for backup power.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Jacksonville?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,500-$13,000 installed in Jacksonville — slightly below the Florida average due to lower labor rates compared to South Florida.

faqSpecific.q: Does JEA have specific generator requirements?
faqSpecific.a: JEA requires standard interconnection compliance through a transfer switch. No special JEA-specific requirements beyond the Florida Building Code. Your installer coordinates the permit with Duval County.

faqSpecific.q: Is Jacksonville at risk from nor'easters too?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. Jacksonville is far enough north to get hit by winter nor'easters as well as summer hurricanes. This dual exposure means outage risk extends beyond the June-November hurricane season.

recentEvent: Hurricane Irma (2017) left over 1 million Broward County customers without power, with some areas waiting 2+ weeks for restoration. More recently, historic flooding in April 2023 dumped 25+ inches of rain on Fort Lauderdale in 24 hours.
costVsState: 5-10% above the Florida average — Broward County is adjacent to Miami-Dade's HVHZ zone, and many coastal properties have enhanced wind and flood requirements
localCode: Broward County follows the Florida Building Code with coastal high-wind provisions. While not in the full HVHZ like Miami-Dade, eastern Broward (Coastal Construction Control Line areas) requires enhanced wind-rated equipment. Flood zone properties are widespread — most of Fort Lauderdale east of I-95 requires elevated generator mounting.
fuelReason: FPL provides gas service throughout Broward County. Most suburban Fort Lauderdale homes have natural gas. Propane is used in some waterfront and island properties.
description: Fort Lauderdale sits on Florida's Gold Coast, directly in the path of Atlantic hurricanes. Broward County's low elevation and extensive canal system make it exceptionally vulnerable to both hurricane surge and rain-driven flooding. FPL serves the area, and Broward consistently ranks among the hardest-hit counties during major storms. The April 2023 flooding event — outside hurricane season — showed that extreme weather risk is year-round.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Fort Lauderdale?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $9,500-$15,000 installed in Fort Lauderdale. This is 5-10% above the Florida average due to Broward County's enhanced coastal requirements and higher labor rates.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need flood-rated mounting in Fort Lauderdale?
faqSpecific.a: Very likely. Most of Fort Lauderdale east of I-95 is in a FEMA flood zone. Your generator must be mounted above the base flood elevation. Your installer will check your specific flood zone designation and determine the required height.

faqSpecific.q: Is Broward County under the same wind code as Miami-Dade?
faqSpecific.a: Not exactly. Broward is adjacent to Miami-Dade's HVHZ but has its own coastal provisions. Eastern Broward (near the coast) has enhanced wind requirements, but they're not as strict as Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ code. Your installer will know the specific requirements for your address.

recentEvent: Hurricane Ian (2022) made landfall near Naples as a Category 4 storm, devastating Collier County. Over 90% of LCEC customers lost power, and restoration took 2-4 weeks across the county.
costVsState: slightly above the Florida average — higher-income market with larger homes and more demand for whole-house coverage
localCode: Collier County follows the Florida Building Code with enhanced wind provisions for the coastal zone. Storm surge risk requires elevated mounting in many waterfront and low-lying areas. Many Naples neighborhoods have HOA restrictions on generator placement and screening requirements.
fuelReason: TECO Peoples Gas serves parts of Naples. Many homes, especially in gated communities, use propane because gas lines don't reach all developments. Propane is more common in Naples than in other Florida metros.
description: Naples took a direct hit from Hurricane Ian in 2022 as a Category 4 storm. The devastation was extreme — over 90% of LCEC customers lost power, storm surge reached 15 feet in some areas, and restoration took weeks. Since Ian, Naples has seen the highest per-capita generator installation rate of any Florida city. With its affluent population, larger homes, and direct Gulf Coast exposure, Naples is a market where whole-house generators are becoming standard rather than optional.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Naples?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $9,500-$15,000 installed in Naples. Larger homes (2,500+ sq ft, common in Naples) often need 24-26 kW, pushing costs to $15,000-$24,000. Naples' higher-end market means more demand for premium Kohler units.

faqSpecific.q: Do Naples HOAs allow generators?
faqSpecific.a: Most Naples HOAs allow standby generators but require them to be placed in rear or side yards with screening (landscape or fence). Some gated communities have specific noise and placement guidelines. Check your HOA covenants before signing an installation contract.

faqSpecific.q: Propane or natural gas in Naples?
faqSpecific.a: It depends on your development. Downtown Naples and some newer communities have TECO Peoples Gas. Many gated communities and older neighborhoods rely on propane. If on propane, a 500-gallon tank is recommended for hurricane preparedness.

primaryRisk: Winter storms and ERCOT grid instability
recentEvent: Winter Storm Uri (2021) left over 2 million DFW homes without power for up to 5 days in sub-freezing temperatures. Pipes burst in thousands of homes that lost heat.
costVsState: close to the Texas average — Dallas has a competitive installer market with lower labor costs than coastal cities
localCode: City of Dallas requires an electrical permit for transfer switch installation. Dallas County follows the NEC with local amendments. No hurricane or flood-specific generator requirements for most of the metro. ERCOT's independent grid is the primary vulnerability.
fuelReason: Atmos Energy serves most of the Dallas metro with cheap, abundant natural gas. Texas gas infrastructure stayed operational during Uri even when the electric grid failed.
description: Dallas doesn't get hurricanes, but Winter Storm Uri in 2021 was worse than most hurricanes for the DFW metro. Over 2 million homes lost power in sub-freezing temperatures for up to 5 days. Pipes burst, homes flooded, and the damage was catastrophic. Since then, generator sales in Dallas have surged. Oncor distributes power in the DFW area, but ERCOT's isolated grid means Texas can't import power from neighboring states when demand exceeds supply.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Dallas?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$13,000 installed in Dallas. The DFW metro has strong installer competition, keeping prices close to the Texas average.

faqSpecific.q: Will my generator protect me during another winter storm like Uri?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. A natural gas standby generator runs independently of the ERCOT grid. During Uri, natural gas supply held up in most DFW areas even when electricity failed. Your furnace, lights, and refrigerator stay on.

faqSpecific.q: Does Dallas have tornado risk too?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. The DFW metro sits at the southern edge of Tornado Alley. North Texas gets 15-20 tornado warnings per year on average. Tornadoes cause sudden, localized power outages with no preparation time.

primaryRisk: ERCOT grid instability and extreme weather
recentEvent: Winter Storm Uri (2021) forced Austin Energy to implement rolling blackouts. Over 200,000 Austin households lost power, many for 3-4 days. Burst pipes caused billions in damage across Travis County.
costVsState: slightly above the Texas average — Austin's booming tech economy drives higher labor rates
localCode: Austin Energy requires notification for generator installations connecting to their grid. Travis County follows the NEC. Austin's rapid growth means permit processing is efficient but can slow during peak construction season.
fuelReason: Texas Gas Service serves the Austin metro. Natural gas is widely available and cheap. Some newer developments in the Hill Country are all-electric, requiring propane for generators.
description: Austin is the fastest-growing generator market in Texas. The city's tech workforce (many working from home) and the Uri experience created an urgency that hasn't faded. Austin Energy is a municipal utility — generally more reliable than the investor-owned utilities — but it still operates within the ERCOT grid and can't avoid system-wide failures. Summer heat waves are increasing grid strain every year.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Austin?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,500-$13,500 installed in Austin. Slightly above the Texas average due to Austin's higher labor rates driven by the booming local economy.

faqSpecific.q: Is Austin Energy more reliable than other Texas utilities?
faqSpecific.a: As a municipal utility, Austin Energy is generally better managed than Oncor or CenterPoint. But it still operates within ERCOT, so system-wide grid failures affect Austin the same as everywhere else in Texas. Uri hit Austin hard.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need a generator for Austin's summer heat?
faqSpecific.a: If you work from home or have family members vulnerable to heat, yes. Austin regularly hits 100°F+ in summer. An extended outage during a heat wave is a genuine health risk. ERCOT's grid has come close to failure during summer peaks multiple times since Uri.

primaryRisk: Extreme heat and ERCOT grid vulnerability
recentEvent: Winter Storm Uri (2021) caused extended rolling blackouts across San Antonio. CPS Energy was heavily criticized for its response, with some neighborhoods losing power for 4+ days in freezing temperatures.
costVsState: slightly below the Texas average — lower cost of living and labor rates compared to Houston and Austin
localCode: CPS Energy requires interconnection compliance for generator installations. Bexar County follows the NEC with standard permitting. No hurricane or flood-specific requirements for most of the metro.
fuelReason: CenterPoint Energy provides gas service in the San Antonio metro. Gas is cheap and widely available.
description: San Antonio's generator market has grown steadily since Uri. CPS Energy, the municipal utility, took heavy criticism for its storm response. The city's extreme summer heat (regularly exceeding 100°F) makes AC during outages a near-medical necessity. San Antonio has lower installation costs than Houston or Austin, making it one of the most affordable Texas metros for generator installation.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in San Antonio?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$12,500 installed in San Antonio — slightly below the Texas average. Lower labor rates and cost of living keep prices accessible.

faqSpecific.q: How reliable is CPS Energy?
faqSpecific.a: CPS Energy was widely criticized after Uri. As a municipal utility operating within ERCOT, it faces the same grid-level vulnerabilities as the rest of Texas. A standby generator is the only guaranteed backup regardless of CPS's reliability.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need a generator for San Antonio's heat?
faqSpecific.a: If you can't tolerate 100°F+ without AC for 24-48 hours, yes. San Antonio's summer heat is dangerous for elderly residents, children, and pets. An extended outage during a July heat wave is not something you want to experience without backup power.

recentEvent: Winter Storm Uri (2021) hit the DFW metro hard. Fort Worth and Tarrant County experienced some of the longest outage durations in the metroplex, with some areas losing power for 5+ days.
costVsState: close to the Texas average — similar market conditions to Dallas with competitive installer pricing
localCode: City of Fort Worth requires an electrical permit for all generator installations. Tarrant County follows the NEC. Standard requirements — no special hurricane or flood codes. Fort Worth's western location puts it closer to Tornado Alley.
fuelReason: Atmos Energy serves the Fort Worth metro. Natural gas is abundant and affordable throughout North Texas.
description: Fort Worth mirrors Dallas in generator demand — Uri was the catalyst. Tarrant County experienced some of the worst outage durations in the DFW metro during the 2021 freeze. Fort Worth also sits closer to Tornado Alley than Dallas, adding another dimension of severe weather risk. The installer market is competitive across the DFW metro, keeping prices reasonable.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Fort Worth?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,000-$13,000 installed in Fort Worth. Pricing is virtually identical to Dallas — both cities share the DFW installer market.

faqSpecific.q: Is Fort Worth at higher tornado risk than Dallas?
faqSpecific.a: Slightly. Fort Worth sits further west, closer to the traditional Tornado Alley corridor. Tarrant County averages more tornado warnings per year than Dallas County. Tornadoes cause instant, localized outages with zero preparation time.

faqSpecific.q: Can I use the same installer for Fort Worth and Dallas?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. Most DFW-area installers serve the entire metroplex (Dallas, Fort Worth, Arlington, Plano, Frisco, etc.). Getting quotes from 2-3 DFW installers gives you good price competition.

recentEvent: Hurricane Harvey (2017) devastated the Coastal Bend. Corpus Christi experienced Category 4 winds and widespread outages. AEP Texas restoration took 2-3 weeks in some areas. More recently, Hurricane Hanna (2020) also caused significant outages.
costVsState: close to the Texas average — coastal location doesn't add significant code costs in Texas like it does in Florida
localCode: Nueces County follows the NEC and Texas local amendments. Coastal flood zone properties along the bay and Mustang Island require elevated mounting. No specific wind-rating requirements like Florida's code, but the Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA) may require documented installation for coverage.
fuelReason: Southern Union Gas serves Corpus Christi. Natural gas is widely available. Propane is used on Mustang Island and in rural Nueces County areas.
description: Corpus Christi is ground zero for Gulf Coast hurricane risk in Texas. Hurricane Harvey's direct hit in 2017 as a Category 4 storm was a defining moment for the Coastal Bend. AEP Texas serves the area, and extended outages after tropical systems are routine. The city's location on the Gulf makes it one of the highest-priority generator markets in Texas.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Corpus Christi?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $8,500-$13,500 installed in Corpus Christi. Close to the Texas average — Texas doesn't add the same coastal code surcharges that Florida does.

faqSpecific.q: Do I need flood-rated mounting in Corpus Christi?
faqSpecific.a: If your property is in a FEMA flood zone (common along Corpus Christi Bay, Oso Bay, and Mustang Island), yes. Elevated mounting adds $300-$600 to the installation.

faqSpecific.q: Does TWIA affect my generator installation?
faqSpecific.a: The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA) provides wind/hail coverage for coastal Texas properties. Having a properly documented generator installation can help with TWIA claims after a storm. Make sure your installer provides complete documentation.

recentEvent: Hurricane Ida (2021) caused widespread outages across the Baton Rouge metro. While the capital fared better than New Orleans, over 400,000 Entergy Louisiana customers in the region lost power for up to 2 weeks.
costVsState: slightly below the Louisiana average — Baton Rouge has lower labor costs and fewer flood zone requirements than New Orleans
localCode: East Baton Rouge Parish follows the Louisiana State Uniform Construction Code. Standard electrical permit required. Flood zone properties along the Mississippi and Amite rivers require elevated mounting. The 2016 Baton Rouge flood highlighted the need for flood-resistant installations.
fuelReason: Entergy provides gas service throughout the Baton Rouge metro. Natural gas is cheap and widely available across East Baton Rouge Parish.
description: Baton Rouge is Louisiana's capital and second-largest city. While it doesn't get the direct coastal hits that New Orleans does, hurricane remnants and tropical storms routinely cause extended outages. The 2016 Baton Rouge flood and Hurricane Ida (2021) both demonstrated that the city's infrastructure is vulnerable. Entergy Louisiana serves the area, and outage duration is consistently above the national average.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Baton Rouge?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $7,500-$12,000 installed in Baton Rouge — slightly below the Louisiana average due to lower labor costs and fewer flood zone requirements than New Orleans.

faqSpecific.q: Is Baton Rouge at flood risk for generator installations?
faqSpecific.a: Properties along the Mississippi River, Amite River, and in areas affected by the 2016 flood may be in FEMA flood zones requiring elevated mounting. Check your flood zone designation with East Baton Rouge Parish.

faqSpecific.q: How does Baton Rouge compare to New Orleans for outages?
faqSpecific.a: Baton Rouge has fewer and shorter outages than New Orleans on average, but it's still well above the national median. Ida caused 2-week outages in Baton Rouge vs. 6+ weeks in New Orleans. Both cities need backup power.

recentEvent: The February 2021 ice storm (concurrent with Winter Storm Uri) coated North Louisiana in ice, downing trees and power lines across Shreveport. Over 100,000 SWEPCO customers lost power for up to a week.
costVsState: below the Louisiana average — lower cost of living, fewer code requirements, and no coastal/flood premiums
localCode: Caddo Parish follows the Louisiana State Uniform Construction Code. Standard electrical permit required. No flood zone or hurricane-specific requirements for most of the Shreveport metro. Straightforward permitting process.
fuelReason: CenterPoint Energy provides gas service in the Shreveport-Bossier metro. Natural gas is cheap and universal in the area.
description: Shreveport's outage risk profile is completely different from coastal Louisiana. Instead of hurricanes, North Louisiana faces ice storms, tornadoes, and severe thunderstorms. The February 2021 ice storm was Shreveport's version of Uri — extensive tree damage, widespread outages, and a week without power for some neighborhoods. SWEPCO serves the area, and installation costs are the lowest in Louisiana thanks to minimal code complexity and lower labor rates.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Shreveport?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $7,000-$11,500 installed in Shreveport — the lowest in Louisiana. No coastal or flood premiums, simpler permitting, and lower labor rates.

faqSpecific.q: Does Shreveport get hurricanes?
faqSpecific.a: Rarely directly, but tropical storm remnants can push through North Louisiana. Shreveport's bigger risks are ice storms (winter) and severe thunderstorms/tornadoes (spring/summer). The threat profile is more like Arkansas or East Texas than coastal Louisiana.

faqSpecific.q: Is SWEPCO reliable?
faqSpecific.a: SWEPCO is adequate in normal conditions but struggles with ice storms. Tree-related outages are common in Shreveport's heavily wooded neighborhoods. When ice coats the lines, SWEPCO restoration can take 3-7 days.

electricityProvider: SLEMCO (Southwest Louisiana Electric Membership Corporation) / Cleco
recentEvent: Hurricanes Laura and Delta hit Southwest Louisiana in 2020 within 6 weeks of each other. While Lafayette avoided the worst damage, both storms caused multi-day outages across Lafayette Parish.
costVsState: close to the Louisiana average — moderate labor rates with some flood zone considerations
localCode: Lafayette Parish follows the Louisiana State Uniform Construction Code. Standard electrical permit required. Some areas in the southern part of the parish (toward Vermilion Bay) are in flood zones requiring elevated mounting.
fuelReason: Centerpoint Energy and local providers serve Lafayette with natural gas. Propane is common in rural Acadiana communities outside the city limits.
description: Lafayette is the heart of Cajun Country and sits in Louisiana's hurricane transition zone — close enough to the coast to feel major storms, far enough inland to avoid the worst storm surge. The back-to-back 2020 hurricane season (Laura and Delta within 6 weeks) demonstrated that Lafayette's exposure is real. SLEMCO and Cleco serve the area, and both cooperatives have invested in grid hardening since 2020.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Lafayette?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $7,500-$12,000 installed in Lafayette. Close to the Louisiana average. SLEMCO and Cleco customers both need standard transfer switch installations.

faqSpecific.q: Did Laura and Delta affect Lafayette directly?
faqSpecific.a: Yes. While the worst damage was further west (Lake Charles), both storms caused multi-day outages across Lafayette Parish. The back-to-back hits in 2020 drove a surge in generator purchases across Acadiana.

faqSpecific.q: SLEMCO or Cleco — does it matter for generators?
faqSpecific.a: No. Both are electric cooperatives and both require standard interconnection through a transfer switch. Your installer handles the coordination regardless of which utility serves your property.

recentEvent: Hurricane Laura (2020) made landfall near Lake Charles as a Category 4 storm, the strongest to hit Louisiana in over 160 years. Over 95% of Calcasieu Parish lost power. Just 6 weeks later, Hurricane Delta hit the same area. Full power restoration took over a month.
costVsState: close to the Louisiana average — despite high demand, competitive pricing from the influx of installers post-Laura
localCode: Calcasieu Parish follows the Louisiana State Uniform Construction Code. Coastal zone properties require elevated mounting. After Laura, many Lake Charles homeowners now opt for hurricane-rated mounting even when not technically required by code.
fuelReason: Entergy provides gas service in the Lake Charles metro. The industrial corridor along the I-10 corridor ensures reliable gas infrastructure. Propane is used in rural Calcasieu Parish.
description: Lake Charles has the highest per-capita generator demand in Louisiana. After Hurricane Laura devastated the city as a Category 4 in 2020 and Hurricane Delta followed 6 weeks later, residents who didn't have generators vowed never to go through that again. Lake Charles averages 80+ hours of outages per year — the highest of any city in our database. Entergy's infrastructure in Calcasieu Parish has been rebuilt since Laura, but the hurricane exposure hasn't changed.

faqSpecific.q: How much does a 22 kW generator cost in Lake Charles?
faqSpecific.a: A 22 kW generator costs $7,500-$12,000 installed in Lake Charles. Despite high demand, the post-Laura influx of installers has kept pricing competitive.

faqSpecific.q: How bad were Laura and Delta for Lake Charles?
faqSpecific.a: Catastrophic. Laura hit as a Category 4 in August 2020, the strongest hurricane to strike Louisiana in 160+ years. Over 95% of Calcasieu Parish lost power. Six weeks later, Delta hit the same area. Full restoration took over a month. Many Lake Charles families were without power for the entire fall of 2020.

faqSpecific.q: Is Lake Charles the most at-risk city in Louisiana?
faqSpecific.a: For direct hurricane hits, arguably yes. Lake Charles sits on the Gulf Coast in Southwest Louisiana, directly in the path of storms entering from the western Gulf. The city has been hit by major hurricanes more frequently than New Orleans in recent decades.
